entry.title: Post RE-FX Valuation without Controlling Object
entry.content:
Hello dear SAP Community,

I am faced with a unique requirement to post IFRS16 related expenses to P&L Accounts for which no Cost Elements are maintained in FI-CO. I am aware that this is against Best Practices and also imposes problems beyond the posting of contract valuations.
Can somebody here think about an approach to post to these expenses to these P&Ls without having to create the Cost Elements or the development of a customization?

Thank you all in advance.

Best Regards
Ben

entry.title: Re: CCM - Copy Multiple Contract at a time
entry.content:
Hi Arindam,

1. WB2R_EXTENSION

2. WB2R_AB_DOCS

Condition Contract Extension

Use

The report extends the selected condition contracts.

The extension is controlled by the parameters on the selection screen. For further information see their documentation.

Integration

Business volume base entries with validity are only taken over if the valid-to date was the final date of the contract. The validity restriction itself is not taken over.

Condition Types with the following condition usages are not taken over into the new contract:

  • Settlement Condition Adjustment
  • Other Contract Conditions (Adjustments)

Selection

Condition contracts with an extension calendar are always selected. Contracts without extension calendar are also selected if the extension relevance in the contract type settings of the process variant is set to independent of extension calendar.

entry.title: How to enable PO automatically send to vendor feature?
entry.content:
After creating the PO, it needs to be sent directly to the supplier. Ideally, the initial order should be sent automatically, and if there are any modifications to the order, the revised order can be manually sent by clicking in the system.

This is external-service-bp.cds where I intended to use @odata.draft.enabled

using {cap.apitest as api} from '../db/schema';

service BusinessPartnerService {
    @odata.draft.enabled
    entity BusinessPartners as
        projection on api.BusinessPartners {
            *
        }
}

 This is external-service-bp.js

module.exports = async service => {
	const BusinessPartnerService = await cds.connect.to("API_BUSINESS_PARTNER");

	service.on("READ", "BusinessPartners", async context => {
		return await BusinessPartnerService.run(context.query);
	});
};

 This is schema.cds which makes it possible to use the entitySet in the Fiori-App

using {API_BUSINESS_PARTNER} from '../srv/external/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER';

namespace cap.apitest;

entity BusinessPartners      as
  projection on API_BUSINESS_PARTNER.A_BusinessPartner {
    *
  };

 This is fiori-service.cds where I adjust the UI

using {BusinessPartnerService} from '../../srv/external-service-bp.cds';

annotate BusinessPartnerService.BusinessPartners with @(
    UI          : {
        SelectionFields: [
            BusinessPartner,
            BusinessPartnerName
        ],
        LineItem       : [
            {Value: BusinessPartner},
            {Value: BusinessPartnerName},
            {Value: FirstName},
            {Value: LastName},
            {Value: CreatedByUser},
            {Value: TradingPartner}
        ],
        Facets         : [{
            $Type : 'UI.ReferenceFacet',
            Label : '{i18n>GeneralInformation}',
            Target: '@UI.Identification'
        }],
        Identification : [
            {Value: BusinessPartner},
            {Value: BusinessPartnerName},
            {Value: FirstName},
            {Value: LastName},
            {Value: BusinessPartnerFullName},
            {Value: OrganizationBPName1},
            {Value: OrganizationBPName2},
            {Value: OrganizationBPName3},
            {Value: OrganizationBPName4},
            {Value: OrganizationFoundationDate},
            {Value: OrganizationLiquidationDate}
        ],
        HeaderInfo     : {
            TypeName      : '{i18n>TypeName}',
            TypeNamePlural: '{i18n>TypeNamePlural}',
            Title         : {Value: BusinessPartnerName},
            Description   : {Value: OrganizationBPName1}
        },
        DeleteHidden   : true,
        UpdateHidden   : false
    },
    Capabilities: {
        UpdateRestrictions: {Updatable: true},
        Updatable         : true
    }
);

annotate BusinessPartnerService.BusinessPartnersAlias with @(
    UI          : {
        SelectionFields: [
            BusinessPartner,
            BPAliasPositionNumber
        ],
        LineItem       : [
            {Value: BusinessPartner},
            {Value: BPAliasPositionNumber},
            {Value: BusinessPartnerAliasName}
        ],
        Facets         : [{
            $Type : 'UI.ReferenceFacet',
            Label : '{i18n>GeneralInformation}',
            Target: '@UI.Identification'
        }],
        Identification : [
            {Value: BusinessPartner},
            {Value: BPAliasPositionNumber},
            {Value: BusinessPartnerAliasName}
        ],
        HeaderInfo     : {
            TypeName      : '{i18n>TypeName}',
            TypeNamePlural: '{i18n>TypeNamePlural}',
            Title         : {Value: BusinessPartner},
            Description   : {Value: BusinessPartnerAliasName}
        },
        DeleteHidden   : true,
        UpdateHidden   : false
    },
    Capabilities: {
        UpdateRestrictions: {Updatable: true},
        Updatable         : true
    }
);

 I hope I provided all necessary information to help you help me. I can't find a proper solution to access CRUD operations for a ODataV2 Service. I also tried using @Rizing/cds-extension, but gave up after I found out that the latest version of @sap/cds makes it unusable.

Do I have a chance to have an Edit Button in the Details View of a Business Partner?

entry.title: How to increase timeout in powershell script while invoking BTP iflow using Invoke-WebRequest?
entry.content:
Hi All,
I am using PowerShell script Invoke-WebRequest command to trigger an SAP BTP iFlow. When the iflow processes quickly (less than 2 minutes), the PowerShell script receives the correct status. However, if the iflow takes longer than 2 minutes, even though you've set a timeout of 1 hour, the script fails with a "Termination Error (Invoke-webRequest): The connection closed Unexpectedly" message

Thanks 
Himanshu

entry.title: How can I print a check after issuing it using the 'Post Outgoing Check' title?
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

I am experiencing an issue with cheque printing in SAP Public Cloud 2408 using the "Manage Outgoing Cheque" functionality. Below is a detailed description of the steps I followed:

  1. I made a payment to a vendor using the "Post Outgoing Payment" title.
  2. After that, I manually created a cheque through the "Manage Outgoing Cheque" functionality.

However, after creating the cheque, I am unable to find any option to print the cheque.

Could you please investigate this issue and provide a solution or guidance on how to print cheque in Sap?

Looking forward to your prompt assistance.

entry.title: Use Purchase Scheduling agreements LU with outbound deliveries
entry.content:
Hi: My customer wants to use the Scheduling agreement type LU for Stock transport Scheduling agreements. Still, we didn't find the configuration path related to the agreement type LU and the delivery type NL and without that assignment it is not possible to take out the inventory from EWM, do you mind please let us know if it is possible to use this function with Outbound deliveries in public cloud, thanks.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Use Purchase Scheduling agreements LU with outbound d...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

Creating a Stock Transport Scheduling Agreement

When you create a stock transport scheduling agreement, you can schedule the exact delivery dates for a stock transfer.

  1. From the Purchasing menu, select Outline Agreement  Scheduling Agreement  Create  Stock Transport Scheduling Agreement.

  2. Enter the necessary data. The agreement type LU is defined in the standard system. The item category must be U . Enter the validity end date.

    You will find a table of the possible combinations of order type and item category under Stock Transfer Using a Stock Transport Order .

  3. Save the scheduling agreement.

  4. Enter the delivery schedule for the scheduling agreement by choosing Outline Agreement  Scheduling Agreement  Delivery Schedule  Maintain .

For more details you can refer to the below link:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/91b21005dded4984bcccf4a69ae1300c/0f62bd534f22b44ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

Stock Transport Scheduling Agreement - SAP Community

 

Thanks and Regards

SAP SUPPORT

entry.title: Maximum number of line items reached in entry view (999) - Activate Entry View Summarization
entry.content:
Hello,

While trying to post an invoice we encountered an issue with error message "Maximum number of line items reached in entry view.

Long text of error 

NA F5727

Maximum number of items reached in entry view (long text)

Diagnosis

The maximum number of items in the entry view of a journal entry ('') has been exceeded.

System Response

Entry views in a journal entry have a three-digit item counter that restricts the number.

Procedure

If the documents come from previous applications (Sales and Distribution, Materials Management, Order Settlement, and so on), you can - and should - set summarization of the entry views or even complete suppression of the update of the entry views in Accounting. To do this, go to self-service configuration UI 106716 "Activate Summarization of Entry View". Also read the explanatory help text there.

 

The error message from system is suggesting to activate "Summarization of Entry View" configuration.

Could you please advise on all the potential downsides of this setting, if any? Such as:
- Will some information from postings be lost
- On which level the summarization is performed (which criteria from the line item has to be the same in order for system to summarize it in one line item, and is this criteria something we can additionally modify per our requirements, through standard customizing or through some BADI?)
- Any other downsides/issues that can occur

 

Thanks and regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Maximum number of line items reached in entry view (9...
entry.content:
See help.sap.com documentation on Entry View Summarization

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/3a295f45d9354aa09418530288fc166c.html?locale=en-US

As referenced therein 

"Note: No data is lost if you activate this summarization option. All the information is kept in full detail in the ledger view of the journal entry."

Review documentation in full.

see referenced also in documentation note 3404001 - Local Versions Supported for Entry View Summarization

If you use one (or several) of the following local versions only, you can activate the 'Entry View Summarization' without restrictions

entry.title: Building custom Excel view in SAPUI5 : Technical Part 3
entry.content:
Hi All,

 

This is part 3 of the blog series and focus of this blog will be on making tables editable, option to add new worksheets and applying CSS to change look and feel of it. 

Check out Part 1 and Part 2-  before jumping to this blog.

After completing Part 1 and 2, we were able to achieve file upload, data parsing and generate tabs, tables dynamically, Tabs at bottom, basic toolbar and Borders.

Ok, this looks good enough for start.

But, It should be editable at least !! 

    Excel is Editable, right?

    It allows to Create new tabs.

    Too much space around cells, remove that.

 

lets try to add these features.

1. Make tables Editable

Earlier, while generating tables when file gets uploaded, we set sap.m.Label to display headers and sap.m.Text to display data.

Instead of Labels and Text, Use sap.m.Input to make it editable. That's all is needed !! 

Replace existing code with below snippet. ( for reference I have commented out old code and below it, placed new code ) 

Function to be edited is "generateTablewithTabsxlsx" which was created in Part 1 of this blog series.

 

 

generateTablewithTabsxlsx: function () {
... old code ...
// loop over all worksheets stored in below array
// for each worksheet => add a table
//                       for each table => add columns and Cells
$.each(aTabs, function (i, val) {
    oTable = new Table({
        fixedLayout: false,
        backgroundDesign: Solid,
        showNoData: true
    });
    var oModelData = oModel.getProperty("/" + val + "/");
    var aColumns = Object.keys(oModelData[0]);
    var oColumnNames = [];
    $.each(aColumns, function (i, value) {
        oColumnNames.push({
            Text: aColumns[i]
        });
    });
    var header = that.getView().getModel("header");
    header.setProperty("/" + val + "/", oColumnNames);
    var oTemplate = new Column({
        // --------- old code commented ---------------
        // header: new Label({
        //     text: "{header>Text}",
        //     customData: [
        //         new CustomData({
        //             key: "colName",
        //             value: "{header>Text}",
        //             writeToDom: true
        //         })
        //     ],
        // })
        // --------- below is the New code snippet ---------------
        header: new Input({
            value: "{header>Text}",
            customData: [
                new CustomData({
                    key: "colName",
                    value: "{header>Text}",
                    writeToDom: true
                })
            ],
        })
    });

    oTable.bindAggregation("columns", "header>/" + val + "/", oTemplate);
    var oItemTemplate = new ColumnListItem();


    $.each(aColumns, function (j, value) {
        var oHeaderName = value;
        // --------- Old code commented out ---------------
        // oItemTemplate.addCell(new Text({
        //     text: "{data>" + oHeaderName + "}"
        // }));
        // --------- below is the New code snippet ---------------
        oItemTemplate.addCell(new Input({
            value: "{data>" + oHeaderName + "}"
        }));
    });
    oTable.bindAggregation("items", {
        path: "data>/" + val + "/",
        template: oItemTemplate
    });


    // Add IconTabFilter for the current worksheet 
    // add the table, created in current loop, as content of IconTabFilter 
    oITB.addItem(
        new IconTabFilter({
            text: val,
            content: new ScrollContainer({
                height: "350px",
                vertical: true,
                content: oTable
            })
        })
    );

});

... old code ...
}

 

 

 

It should look like this..

Add custom CSS to hide boxes and underline effect of Input control.

 

 

.sapMInputBaseContentWrapper:not(.sapMInputBaseReadonlyWrapper):not(.sapMInputBaseContentWrapperState)
{
    box-shadow: none;
}

.sapMInputBaseContentWrapper{
    background: none;
}

 

 

After that, it should look like ..

Table looks good but what happend to the Combobox ?

Digression over overriding UI5 standard class 

If you look at Combo Box at top closely, it is transparent now. CSS has affected other UI5 controls as well.

Whenever custom CSS style or effect is needed, make sure to add a custom class or an ID to the control and restrict CSS style to that control only.

Since all dynamically generated content is inside IconTabBar and it has a custom class, apply it to Custom CSS styling to restrict it to UI5 contols contained by IconTabBar.

 

 

<IconTabBar id="id_itb" class="excel">

 

 

 

Custom CSS restricted to Icon Tab Bar. To do so, CSS Combinators are used.

Check out more details about CSS Combinators here. 

Specific Controls which are descendents of a control with class "excel". ( just add space after .excel )

 

 

.excel .sapMListTblCell {
    border-left: 1px solid #ccc;
}

.excel .sapMInputBaseContentWrapper:not(.sapMInputBaseReadonlyWrapper):not(.sapMInputBaseContentWrapperState)
{
    box-shadow: none;
}

.excel .sapMInputBaseContentWrapper{
    background: none;
}

 

 

 

after that, it should look like ..

Now, no custom style is applied on Combobox ( cause it is not in scope of .excel class )
 

2. While scrolling, Headers should stick to top

 

 

generateTablewithTabsxlsx: function () {
... old code as it is ...
oTable = new sap.m.Table({
    fixedLayout: false,
    backgroundDesign: sap.m.BackgroundDesign.Solid,
    sticky : [sap.m.Sticky.HeaderToolbar,
              sap.m.Sticky.InfoToolbar,
              sap.m.Sticky.ColumnHeaders],
    showNoData: true
});
... old code as it is ...
}

 

 

 

This will make sure that while scrolling content, header sticks to the top.

It should look like this..

Header ( First Row ) is sticky and is not moving while scrolling.

 

Optional : If you don't want extra padding that is applied to all cells, add below styles in CSS file.

 

 

.excel .sapMColumnHeader,

.excel .sapMListTblCell,

.excel .sapMListTblHighlightCol+.sapMListTblHeaderCell,

.excel .sapMListTblHighlightCell+.sapMListTblCell,

.sapUiSizeCompact .excel .sapMListTblHighlightCol+.sapMListTblCell.sapMListTblHeaderCell

{
padding: 0;
}

 

 

 

and it would look like..

 

3. Option to create Tabs

update old code to add dummy entry at the end of all worksheets with "+" sign.

 

 

generateTablewithTabsxlsx: function () {

    .. old code as it is..

    // loop over all worksheets stored in below array
    // for each worksheet => add a table
    //                       for each table => add columns and Cells
    $.each(aTabs, function (i, val) {
    .. old code as it is ..
    });

    // ---------- new code below ----------
    // dummy entry at end of actual worksheets 
    oITB.addItem(
        new IconTabFilter({
            text: "+",
            key: "+"                                                
        })
    );

},

 

 

 

Add new function onClickNew as below. This will create and insert new empty worksheet tabs when "+" is clicked.

 

 

onClickNew: function(oEvent){
    debugger;
    
    var sKey = oEvent.getParameters().key;
    var oITB = that.getView().byId("id_itb");
    var sLen = oITB.getItems().length;
    
    if (sKey == "+") {                    
        if(!that.counter){
            that.counter = 0;
        }
        that.counter += 1;
        // do not expand
        // add new filter bar before current position
        oITB.insertItem(
            new IconTabFilter({
                text: "New Tab",
                key: that.counter,
                content: new ScrollContainer({
                    height: "350px",
                    vertical: true,
                    content: new Text({
                        text : "hi"
                    })
                })
            })
            , sLen - 1);
        oITB.setSelectedKey(that.counter);
        

    }
}

 

 

Make sure to add "select" event handler "onClickNew" for the IconTabBar defined in the View.

Whenever any of the IconTabFilters inside IconTabBar is clicked, select event is fired. 

 

.. old code as it is ..
<IconTabBar id="id_itb" class="excel" select="onClickNew">
.. old code as it is ..

 

 

It should look like..

 

Add below code to add an empty table when new worksheet is added. 

 

 

onClickNew: function (oEvent) {

var oModel = that.getView().getModel("data");
var aWorkSheet = oModel.oData;

var sKey = oEvent.getParameters().key;
var oITB = that.getView().byId("id_itb");
var sLen = oITB.getItems().length;

if (sKey == "+") {
    if (!that.counter) {
    that.counter = 0;
    }
    that.counter += 1;
    var sNewWorksheet = that.counter;
    aWorkSheet[sNewWorksheet] = [
    {
        column1: "",
        column2: "",
        column3: "",
        column4: "",
        column5: "",
    },
    {
        column1: "",
        column2: "",
        column3: "",
        column4: "",
        column5: "",
    },
    {
        column1: "",
        column2: "",
        column3: "",
        column4: "",
        column5: "",
    },
    {
        column1: "",
        column2: "",
        column3: "",
        column4: "",
        column5: "",
    },
    ];

    var val = that.counter;
    var oTable = new Table({
    fixedLayout: false,
    backgroundDesign: Solid,
    sticky: [
        HeaderToolbar,
        InfoToolbar,
        ColumnHeaders,
    ],
    showNoData: true,
    });
    var oModelData = oModel.getProperty("/" + val + "/");
    var aColumns = Object.keys(oModelData[0]);
    var oColumnNames = [];
    $.each(aColumns, function (i, value) {
    oColumnNames.push({
        Text: aColumns[i],
    });
    });
    var header = that.getView().getModel("header");
    header.setProperty("/" + val + "/", oColumnNames);
    var oTemplate = new Column({
    header: new Input({
        value: "{header>Text}",
        customData: [
        new CustomData({
            key: "colName",
            value: "{header>Text}",
            writeToDom: true,
        }),
        ],
    }),
    });

    oTable.bindAggregation("columns", "header>/" + val + "/", oTemplate);
    var oItemTemplate = new ColumnListItem();

    $.each(aColumns, function (j, value) {
    var oHeaderName = value;
    oItemTemplate.addCell(
        new Input({
        value: "{data>" + oHeaderName + "}",
        })
    );
    });
    oTable.bindAggregation("items", {
    path: "data>/" + val + "/",
    template: oItemTemplate,
    });

    // add new filter bar before current position
    oITB.insertItem(
    new IconTabFilter({
        text: "New Tab",
        key: that.counter,
        content: new ScrollContainer({
        height: "350px",
        vertical: true,
        content: oTable,
        }),
    }),
    sLen - 1
    );

    // set selected key to newly created tab
    oITB.setSelectedKey(that.counter);
}
},

 

 

It should look like this..

Thank you for going though the entire blog. 

Will publish next blog soon on exporting all tabs as worksheets of single EXCEL file. 

Stay tuned for upcoming blogs on more features enhancement to this app.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Test Cockpit in the Cloud – How to use baseline
entry.content:
Hi @SylvainA ,

baseline is based on the ATC check results of the ATC runs (not on single objects or findings). You can take ATC check results (based on the projects in Custom Code Migration app) into baseline. An ATC check result which was added to the baseline will not be deleted. If you want to work on the findings of the ATC check run, you can remove it from the baseline, it is again handled like any result.

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: AR & AP Regrouping in SAP (S/4 HANA Finance)
entry.content:
Hi  Shakeel,

My business  would expect a reclass from receivables to payables only when a customer on a net basis shows a credit value  or vendor on net basis  shows a  debit value.  Is this functionality available 

Our testing shows that  Customer with credit balance and vendor with Debit balance are reclassified with ref to groups at assignment level. During the process system checks individual transaction both in customer and vendor ledger and groups it at assignment field as debit/ credit eligible to be reclassified. can  grouping at assignment field can be avoided ? System should just check the ent balance and sane should be reclassified 

Pankaj

entry.title: Re: Is there an Internal Order field available in S4HANA...
entry.content:
Internal orders are not available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition we suggest to use WBS insteads to control account assignments under Enterprise Projects.

The deprecation occured in 1902 (Feb 2019) changing them for Project Controls. You can see information here: https://me.sap.com/notes/2853554/E

entry.title: Re: IAS as central user management system
entry.content:
Hi, 

If you manually delete a user in IAS, the next IPS job will replicate the user to IAS. Once you configure Azure as a corporate IdP, you need to manage your users in Azure. 

For the user delete scenario in Azure, you can add transformation logic to map the 'accountEnabled' flag from Azure to the 'Inactive' flag in IAS. However, the BTP shadow user will still exist.

Rrgards,

Bhagya

entry.title: Re: Free Goods Determination not getting determined in Sa...
entry.content:
Hello @thangarajthomas 

As per the standard, if you want to use the free goods determination process, certain business conditions need to be met:

  • The material must be available in stock.
  • If you are using trading goods, you can directly procure the material

Additionally, from a configuration perspective, the item category determination for free goods is based on usage and the higher-level item category, which cannot be edited in the current version.

Ideally, free goods are not applicable for make-to-order products. 

However, it would be great if you could explain your business requirement.

Thank You

entry.title: How to restrict JNL delivery document in open billing documents list for intra-state STO process
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

Could you please advise on the option to restrict JNL delivery document in the open billing documents list for Intra-State STO process in India. .

We are using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

In India, for STO transactions within state (Intra-State - same region code for plant and customer ), there is no requirement to create billing document. 

However for Inter-state STO transactions (different region code for plant and customer), billing document is required. 

Our requirement is to restrict JNL delivery document (for Intra-state) in VF04 billing due list. 

Could you please advise if there is any BaDI or any other enhancement option available in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud,  to enable this restriction based on same region code for Plant & customer.

VF04 - Maintain Billing Due List

Please advise. 

Thank you

kind regards

Thangaraj Thomas

entry.title: Unlocking Value: Insights from the SAP ALM Summit 2024 EMEA
entry.content:
Anticipation for the SAP ALM Summit EMEA in Mannheim, Germany, had been growing for weeks, and the event week finally arrived. The day before, the Manufaktur venue buzzed with activity as banners were hung, booths were set up, and excitement filled the air. Speakers conducted dry runs to ensure thorough preparation to deliver the best experience for customers and partners. 

When the big day finally arrived, the venue came alive with activity. The attendees started to trickle in, greeted warmly by smiling faces at the registration desk. The aroma of fresh coffee filled the air, providing a comforting backdrop to the keynote sessions. 

The keynotes featured an exciting and fresh approach delivered by multiple industry experts from SAP, SAP partners and customers. The audience quickly became engaged, sitting up, leaning in, and reaching for their phones to capture the moment. 

From the beginning, speakers used various metaphors to explain SAP Cloud ALM, such as “Constant Companion,” and “Guiding Light” which captivated the audience. Explaining a data migration strategy for transitioning from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM on a single napkin is challenging, yet we successfully captured the participants' attention, leaving them eager for more. Our RISE with SAP and Clean Core team colleagues helped us connect the dots regarding SAP’s strategy and how SAP Cloud ALM is the key adoption platform for our customers’ transformations. You will soon find the inspiring session recordings and presentations on our event page. 

 The time is NOW to start planning your transition! 

This Manufaktur venue brought a refreshing, youthful energy to the event. An old cotton ball factory transformed into a modern event space, its industrial chic style embodies the idea of transformation, perfectly encapsulating the occasion. Attendees learned how they can benefit from SAP Cloud ALM for their SAP S/4HANA transition projects and how to align with clean core principles. They also learned how SAP recognizes that transitioning from the cherished SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM can be challenging for many customers, but they will be supported with tools and content that will enable them to adopt SAP’s essential ALM solution for the future. Several sessions covered the planned transition methodology roadmap and selected data transition of content from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM, and customer feedback was positive. 

SAP Cloud ALM APIs 

Attendees experienced real-world examples from our partners and customers that demonstrated how they have consumed, extended, integrated, and complemented their ALM processes. Watching these use cases is a testament to the openness and limitless innovation in the SAP ALM world. One example that captured my attention was the bi-directional Jira to SAP Cloud ALM integration that will evolve into a business-process-based test automation approach for the future. 

SAP Partner NTT “conquered the world” with their winning ALM-athon demonstration!

 The SAP ALM Summit EMEA was more than just an event; it was a collaborative effort where all attendees played a crucial role. Partners showcased their latest solutions, customers shared their success stories, and the ALM community celebrated the ALM-athon winner NTT. Having been part of the SAP ecosystem for two decades, the sense of community keeps me deeply connected.  

Thanks to everyone for all the fulfilling conversations, learning, and sense of belonging, and see you soon at SAP ALM SUMMIT North America at SAP Headquarters (Newtown Square PA) from March 24-26! 

PS: I’ll update this post with the link to the event recordings when it’s available. 

End of the event Celebration

entry.title: Re: SCC1N Usage - Simple Way
entry.content:
Just some comments for the audience reading this topic.  This great explanation by Gopagani  is available for SAP S/4HANA on-premise and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

On SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition this transaction is not available as this is a standard solution and copy of transports into different clients is not possible as you can't create new clients outside of what is delivered standard in a 3 system landscape (Dev (client 100, 80) - Test (100) and Production (100)). In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition in a 3 system landscape, you can off course move transports into the customer system landscape  - Development, Testing and Production. We will also offering a new concept called Parallel Project Line, which will allow our customers to create another line to create additional clients in Development (120) and Testing (120) which they can be used to run testing, data migration example, etc. so you don't do these changes in the main line.  If interested in this concept I suggest to check the following information: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/bee2fd8498b5420696cdca00a83b2ba6/ba3c2949cab04efbb7136bcab7e69ff0.html?q=Parallel%20Project%20Line&locale=en-US

Hope this helps

entry.title: Re: A new generation of CDS views: CDS view entities
entry.content:
Hi AndreaUS,

we have kind of strange behaviour with view entities which lead to some trouble for us. I distilled the essence in this example. It occurs on our S/4 HANA 2022 system.

Given this DDIC based CDS-View

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZTESTDDICCDS'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'test cds'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
define view ztest_ddic_cds
  as select from t000
{
  key mandt,
      mtext,
      mwaer,

      case when t000.mwaer = 'CHF'
        then 'X'
        else ''
      end as is_chf,

      case when t000.mwaer = 'CHF'
      then 'X'
      else ' '
      end as is_chf2
}

 

gives this SQL CREATE statement

 

CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW "ZTESTDDICCDS" AS SELECT 
  "T000"."MANDT", 
  "T000"."MTEXT", 
  "T000"."MWAER", 
  CASE WHEN "T000"."MWAER" = N'CHF' THEN N'X' ELSE N'' 
  END AS "IS_CHF", 
  CASE WHEN "T000"."MWAER" = N'CHF' THEN N'X' ELSE N'' 
  END AS "IS_CHF2" 
FROM "T000" "T000"

 

As a view entity it looks like this:

 

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'test cds'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
  serviceQuality: #X,
  sizeCategory: #S,
  dataClass: #MIXED
}
define view entity ztest_cds
  as select from t000
{
  key mandt,
      mtext,
      mwaer,

      case when t000.mwaer = 'CHF'
        then 'X'
        else ''
      end as is_chf,

      case when t000.mwaer = 'CHF'
      then 'X'
      else ' '
      end as is_chf2
}

 

 gives this SQL CREATE statement

 

CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW "ZTEST_CDS" AS SELECT 
  "T000"."MANDT", 
  "T000"."MTEXT", 
  "T000"."MWAER", 
  CASE WHEN "T000"."MWAER" = N'CHF' THEN N'X' ELSE N'' 
  END AS "IS_CHF", 
  CASE WHEN "T000"."MWAER" = N'CHF' THEN N'X' ELSE NCHAR(32) 
  END AS "IS_CHF2" 
FROM "T000" "T000"

 

As you can see in line the ELSE branch has type NCHAR(32). Now the question is why? This is completely unexpected to us and lead to some trouble in above layers.

Thanks in advance.

BR Christian

entry.title: How the SAP's 'Aspiring Product Manager' Program Helped Me Thrive in My Role
entry.content:
Bridging the Gap: From Technical to Strategic

As a former cloud architect, my focus had always been on technical design, implementation, and ensuring systems were scalable and robust. However, as a product manager, I learned that my primary focus should be on the customer—understanding their needs, behaviors, and pain points. This shift in perspective is something the training program helped me cultivate.

The structured learning sessions exposed me to crucial concepts such as user empathy, strategic roadmapping, and data-driven decision-making. It allowed me to see the bigger picture beyond just solving technical problems: I had to learn how to think about why a product is being built and how it delivers value to the customer.

Key Product Management Skills I Developed

  1. Customer-Centric Thinking
    One of the most significant changes in my mindset was learning to prioritize the customer journey. As part of the training, we dove deep into creating personas, mapping customer journeys, and understanding how to anticipate user needs. This was instrumental when I was tasked with revamping customer journey maps and personas for a greenfield 0-to-1 app, aimed at reducing the time to value (TTV) for our customers from months to minutes.

  2. Defining Product Strategy
    Coming from an architect’s background, I was comfortable with technical design, but I had little experience in defining product vision and strategy. The training covered how to craft a product vision, aligning it with business objectives, and how to make data-informed decisions. These skills are now at the core of my role within the ISBN product strategy team. I’m involved in redesigning the deployment experience of the ISBN suite, which includes automation and self-service features. The training empowered me to contribute more effectively to high-level product discussions.

  3. Cross-functional Collaboration
    Working with diverse teams was not new to me, but leading cross-functional collaboration as a product manager is a different challenge altogether. The training highlighted best practices for communication and collaboration with engineering, UX, sales, marketing, and customer support teams. This has been vital in my current role where I lead initiatives involving multiple stakeholders.

  4. Prioritization and Decision-Making
    With so many moving parts in a product's development, one of the toughest skills to master is prioritization. The program taught us frameworks like the RICE score model (Reach, Impact, Confidence, Effort), which have helped me make informed decisions when balancing customer needs with business goals. This has been especially valuable in launching new features for the ISBN suite, where timelines are tight and resources limited.

The Outcome: Delivering Greater Impact

Since completing the Aspiring Product Manager program, I have been able to approach my responsibilities with a renewed focus. Whether it's defining the product strategy for ISBN or working on a greenfield application, the skills I honed during the training have allowed me to have a greater impact. I’ve gained the confidence to make strategic decisions and now have a clearer understanding of how to lead products that solve real customer problems.

The training also gave me the tools to understand the business side of product management, which was something I hadn’t previously had the opportunity to explore in-depth. This is crucial as we continue to innovate in SAP, and especially as we design new, automated solutions to enhance customer experiences with our products.

Final Thoughts

For anyone with a technical background looking to transition into product management, I can’t recommend the Aspiring Product Manager program enough(available internally only for SAP employees!). It not only provided me with the skills to thrive in my current role but also changed how I approach product challenges—thinking more strategically and always with the customer in mind. My journey as a product manager is just getting started, but with this foundational training, I feel more prepared than ever to take on new challenges and continue driving product success at SAP.

entry.title: Re: Emissão Cloud MDFe Oubound campo issuingDateTime
entry.content:
Olá Bruno, tudo bem?

Essa data/hora deve estar no formato "ISO 8601 Date/Time with time zone", ou seja, "yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss+|–hh:mm", exemplo "2020-07-30T15:30:12-03:00".

Na documentação do SAP Business Accelerator Hub também esse exemplo. Para acessar, expanda o campo na  (setinha) à direita.

Atenciosamente,

Gideão Franco.

entry.title: Re: How to Create Custom CDS View of Customer request
entry.content:
Well, CDS is not required in this case. The utility of CDS is to fetch data from sap tables which is not directly exposed in S/4HC. To create the interface, you need to find the appropriate API from the API hub. The API documentation will have the details about required fields. The mapping between the external fields and the s/4 fidls needs to be done using CPI tool. 

Thank you!
